lede: A look at January 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Nicolas Sarkozy and Jessica Ennis-Hill, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Tropical Storm Domoina
events.desc: Tropical Storm Domoina makes landfall in southern Mozambique, eventually causing 214 deaths and some of the most severe flooding so far recorded in the region.

events.title: Ronald Reagan
events.desc: Ronald Reagan lifts remaining domestic petroleum price and allocation controls in the United States, helping to end the 1979 energy crisis and begin the 1980s oil glut.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Supplies begin to reach the Republic of China over the newly reopened Burma Road.

events.title: Iceland
events.desc: Iceland becomes the first Western country to legalize therapeutic abortion.

events.title: Knickerbocker storm
events.desc: Knickerbocker Storm: Washington, D.C.'s biggest snowfall, causes a disaster when the roof of the Knickerbocker Theatre collapses, killing over 100 people.

events.title: Manitoba
events.desc: The Canadian province of Manitoba grants women the right to vote and run for office in provincial elections (although still excluding women of Indigenous or Asian heritage), marking the first time women in Canada are granted voting rights.

events.title: Cuba
events.desc: United States troops leave Cuba, with the exception of Guantanamo Bay Naval Base, after being there since the Spanish–American War.

events.title: Edward VI
events.desc: Edward VI, the nine-year-old son of Henry VIII, becomes King of England on his father's death.

events.title: Charles VI of France
events.desc: King Charles VI of France is nearly killed when several other dancers' costumes catch fire during a masquerade ball in Paris.

events.title: Robert de Comines
events.desc: Robert de Comines, appointed Earl of Northumbria by William the Conqueror, rides into Durham, England, where he is defeated and killed by rebels. This incident leads to the Harrying of the North.

births.name: Nicolas Sarkozy
births.line: French lawyer and politician, 23rd President of France

births.name: George Hamilton-Gordon, 4th Earl of Aberdeen
births.line: Scottish politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

deaths.text: Joseph Brodsky, Russian-American poet and essayist, Nobel Prize laureate
James Scullin, Australian journalist and politician, 9th Prime Minister of Australia
W. B. Yeats, Irish poet and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate
